stories.title: Create SwiftData Task model
stories.description: Create the core Task @Model with all properties needed for v1. All properties must have defaults or be optional for CloudKit compatibility. Use UUID for identity, never @Attribute(.unique). Verify all SwiftData APIs against Apple documentation before coding.
acceptance_criteria: Task @Model compiles with: id (UUID), title (String), body (Data?), priority (String), isCompleted (Bool), createdAt (Date), completedAt (Date?), parentTask (Task?), folder (Folder?), drawingData (Data?), calendarEventId (String?)
All properties have default values or are optional
No @Attribute(.unique) used anywhere
Project builds without errors in Xcode

stories.title: Create SwiftData Attachment model
stories.description: Create the Attachment @Model for file/photo/audio attachments. Must relate to Task optionally. Same CloudKit constraints apply.
acceptance_criteria: Attachment @Model compiles with: id (UUID), type (String), data (Data?), fileName (String?), createdAt (Date), task (Task?)
Relationship to Task is optional
Project builds without errors

stories.title: Create SwiftData Folder model
stories.description: Create the Folder @Model for task organization (v2 feature, but model needed now for schema). Must support nesting (parent folder reference).
acceptance_criteria: Folder @Model compiles with: id (UUID), name (String), parentFolder (Folder?), createdAt (Date)
Relationship to parent folder is optional (supports nesting)
Project builds without errors

stories.title: Configure ModelContainer and app entry point
stories.description: Update Note_takingApp.swift to configure SwiftData ModelContainer with Task, Attachment, and Folder models. Set up for future CloudKit sync (cloudKitDatabase: .automatic comes in Phase 3).
acceptance_criteria: ModelContainer configured with Task, Attachment, Folder schemas
App launches successfully in simulator
Project builds without errors

stories.title: Create Asset Catalog priority colors
stories.description: Define 3 priority colors in Assets.xcassets with light AND dark mode variants: PriorityHigh (red), PriorityMedium (orange), PriorityDefault (gray). Create Color+App.swift extension for clean access.
acceptance_criteria: 3 color sets exist in Asset Catalog with Any and Dark appearance variants
Color+App.swift provides Color.priorityHigh, Color.priorityMedium, Color.priorityDefault
Colors render correctly in both dark and light mode previews
Project builds without errors

stories.title: Build Task List screen — empty state and list view
stories.description: Replace ContentView with the main Task List screen. Show a scrollable list of tasks. Each task row shows: checkbox (left), title text (center), priority color marker (right). Empty state shows just the + button. Use SF Symbols for all icons. Follow Apple HIG spacing (16-20pt margins, 8pt between items, 44pt tap targets).
acceptance_criteria: App opens directly to task list — no splash, no onboarding
Empty state shows + button only — clean and inviting
Task rows display: checkbox circle (left), title (center), color marker square (right), '...' below
SF Symbols used: circle for checkbox, plus.circle.fill for add button
App renders correctly in SwiftUI preview

stories.title: Implement inline task creation (+ button)
stories.description: Add + button at bottom right. Tapping creates a new task row inline with cursor focused for immediate typing. New task starts with gray (default) priority. Like Apple Reminders behavior.
acceptance_criteria: '+' button visible at bottom right of task list
Tapping '+' creates new task row with cursor focused
New task saved to SwiftData with default priority (gray)
Tap area is at least 44x44pt
Works in simulator

stories.title: Implement task completion (checkbox tap)
stories.description: Tapping the checkbox on a task: strikethrough text, fade animation, then disappear from active list. Task marked isCompleted=true with completedAt timestamp. Haptic feedback on completion.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping checkbox toggles isCompleted to true
Task gets strikethrough text + fade animation
Task disappears from active list after animation
completedAt timestamp set on completion
Haptic feedback fires on tap (.sensoryFeedback)

stories.title: Implement priority color marker
stories.description: Small colored square on each task row. Tapping shows a minimal picker with 3 color options (red, orange, gray). Color changes immediately. Tap area 44x44pt.
acceptance_criteria: Color marker square visible on each task row
Tapping marker shows picker with 3 colors
Selecting a color updates task immediately
Tap area is at least 44x44pt
Colors use Asset Catalog PriorityHigh/Medium/Default

stories.title: Implement swipe gestures (indent + delete)
stories.description: Swipe right on a task to indent it (make sub-task of task above). Swipe far right to delete. Use SwiftUI .swipeActions or custom DragGesture. Indented tasks show visual nesting with padding.
acceptance_criteria: Swipe right indents task (sets parentTask to task above)
Swipe far right deletes task from SwiftData
Indented tasks visually nested with increased leading padding
Animations are smooth and native-feeling

stories.title: Implement settings menu (Sort By, Show Completed, Theme)
stories.description: Gear icon in top-right of task list. Tapping opens dropdown with: Sort By (Creation Date / Priority), Show Completed toggle, Theme entry point. Sort By changes task list ordering. Show Completed reveals/hides completed tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Gear icon (SF Symbol: gearshape) visible in top-right area
Tapping opens menu with Sort By, Show Completed, Theme
Sort By toggles between creation date and priority ordering
Show Completed toggle reveals/hides completed tasks in list
Theme option exists but opens placeholder (US-6.2)

stories.title: Build Task Detail page — navigation and header
stories.description: Tapping '...' on a task navigates to the Task Detail page. Shows: back arrow (top-left), auto-generated creation date, share button, checkmark (hide/show toolbar). Back arrow returns to Task List. Uses NavigationStack.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping '...' opens TaskDetailView via NavigationStack
Back arrow (chevron.left) returns to task list
Auto-date displayed: format '26 March 2026 at 5:45PM'
Share button (square.and.arrow.up) visible in top bar
Checkmark button (checkmark.circle) visible in top bar

stories.title: Integrate MarkupEditor for rich text editing
stories.description: Add MarkupEditor Swift Package dependency. Embed MarkupEditor view in TaskDetailView for WYSIWYG editing. Save editor content to task body (Data). Must verify MarkupEditor API against its GitHub documentation before coding.
acceptance_criteria: MarkupEditor added as Swift Package dependency
Editor view renders in TaskDetailView
User can type and format text (bold, italic, underline, strikethrough)
Editor content saved to task.body on changes
Project builds without errors

stories.title: Build editor toolbar with 6 buttons
stories.description: Create the formatting toolbar above the keyboard with 6 buttons matching Apple Notes layout: Aa (text formatting), Checklist, Table, Attachment (paperclip), Markup (drawing), Paragraph (indent/list). Toolbar orders by Most Recently Used (MRU) — last used tool moves to leftmost position. Use SF Symbols for all icons.
acceptance_criteria: Toolbar shows 6 buttons with SF Symbols: textformat, checklist, tablecells, paperclip, pencil.tip.crop.circle, list.bullet
Toolbar appears above keyboard when editing
Most Recently Used tool moves to leftmost position
Each button triggers its respective formatting action or opens sub-view
Toolbar can be hidden/shown via checkmark button

stories.title: Integrate PencilKit for drawing/handwriting
stories.description: Wrap PKCanvasView in UIViewRepresentable. Show PKToolPicker when markup button is tapped. Save PKDrawing as Data in task.drawingData. Works with Apple Pencil and finger. Verify PencilKit APIs against Apple documentation.
acceptance_criteria: PKCanvasView wrapped in UIViewRepresentable
PKToolPicker shows pen, pencil, marker, eraser, color picker
Drawing saved to task.drawingData as encoded PKDrawing
Works with finger input (no Apple Pencil required for testing)
Toggle between text editing and drawing mode

stories.title: Implement date detection and blue highlighting
stories.description: Use NSDataDetector to scan text for dates as user types (debounced ~300ms). Detected dates highlighted in blue. This works within or alongside the MarkupEditor text. Verify NSDataDetector API against Apple documentation.
acceptance_criteria: NSDataDetector configured with .date type
Scanning runs on text change with ~300ms debounce
Detected dates (e.g., 'March 30', 'next Friday') highlighted in blue
Non-date text remains unaffected
Works with common date formats

stories.title: Implement EventKit calendar sync for detected dates
stories.description: When a date is detected in task text, silently create an EKEvent in Apple Calendar via EventKit. No push notifications (no alarms). Store eventIdentifier in task.calendarEventId for duplicate prevention. Request calendar permission gracefully. Verify EventKit APIs against Apple documentation.
acceptance_criteria: NSCalendarsFullAccessUsageDescription added to Info.plist
EKEventStore requests permission on first use
Detected dates create EKEvent with task title, no alarms
eventIdentifier stored in task.calendarEventId (prevents duplicates)
Graceful degradation if permission denied (dates still highlighted)

stories.title: Implement system theme support (dark/light)
stories.description: Ensure entire app follows system dark/light mode. Use semantic colors throughout (Color.primary, Color.secondary, Color(.systemBackground)). Never hardcode Color.white, Color.black, Color.gray. Liquid Glass effects come automatically with iOS 26.
acceptance_criteria: App renders correctly in dark mode
App renders correctly in light mode
No hardcoded Color.white, Color.black, or Color.gray in codebase
All text uses Color.primary or Color.secondary
All backgrounds use Color(.systemBackground) or semantic equivalents

stories.title: Configure iCloud sync (SwiftData + CloudKit)
stories.description: Enable iCloud sync via SwiftData + CloudKit. Add iCloud capability, CloudKit container, Background Modes. Set cloudKitDatabase: .automatic. Must test on real device. Verify SwiftData CloudKit APIs against Apple documentation.
acceptance_criteria: iCloud capability added with CloudKit container
Background Modes enabled with Remote Notifications
ModelContainer uses cloudKitDatabase: .automatic
initializeCloudKitSchema() called for schema sync
Project builds without errors (real device testing deferred)

stories.title: Implement Siri App Intents (direct voice creation)
stories.description: Create AppIntent struct for 'Create Task' action. Adopt Assistant Schema for flexible natural language. Register with AppShortcutsProvider. Siri captures title and optional details. Must study WWDC24/25 sessions and verify App Intents APIs against Apple documentation. Never hardcode phrases.
acceptance_criteria: CreateTaskIntent struct conforms to AppIntent protocol
Intent has title parameter and optional details parameter
perform() method creates Task in SwiftData
AppShortcutsProvider registers the intent
Project builds without errors

stories.title: Implement Siri Reminders auto-import
stories.description: Create a service that watches Apple Reminders via EventKit (EKReminder). When a new reminder is created (e.g., via 'Hey Siri, remind me to...'), auto-import it as a Task. Add settings toggle to enable/disable. Optional auto-delete of Apple Reminder after import. Verify EventKit Reminders APIs against Apple documentation.
acceptance_criteria: NSRemindersFullAccessUsageDescription added to Info.plist
Service watches for new EKReminders
New reminders auto-imported as Tasks in SwiftData
Settings toggle: 'Import from Siri & Apple Reminders'
Graceful handling if Reminders permission denied

stories.title: Build Theme page skeleton
stories.description: Create a placeholder Theme page accessible from settings menu. Shows 'Themes coming soon' message. UI skeleton ready for v2 monetization features.
acceptance_criteria: Theme option in settings menu navigates to ThemeView
ThemeView shows placeholder message
Navigation works (back to settings/task list)
UI is clean and matches app design

stories.title: Implement attachments (scan, photo, audio, file)
stories.description: Paperclip button in editor toolbar opens attachment menu. Options: Scan Text (VisionKit), Scan Documents, Take Photo/Video, Choose Photo/Video, Record Audio, Attach File. Attachments saved as Attachment @Model in SwiftData. Verify all framework APIs against Apple documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Paperclip button opens attachment menu with 6 options
At least photo picker (PHPicker) works end-to-end
Selected attachment saved as Attachment @Model linked to Task
Attachment data syncs via SwiftData
Menu uses SF Symbols for each option icon
